original: Conrad I of Germany [Conrad the Younger] (30) elected as King of East Francia
Battle of Varna, Black Sea: Sultan Murad II defeats the Crusaders
John I Zapolyai of Transylvania chosen as King of Hungary
Flemish painter Jan Matsys banished from Antwerp for religious beliefs
Battle at St-Denis: French government army vs Huguenots
William Louis of Nassau appointed Stadtholder of Friesland
Failed palace revolution in France against Cardinal Richelieu
Dutch formally cede New Netherland (New York) to the English
Pope Innocent XI publishes decree Coelestis pastor
English parliament accept army reduction
The last Colonial governor of New Jersey, William Franklin, signs the charter of Queen's College (later renamed Rutgers University).
Second Continental Congress forms the Continental Marines, precursor to the United States Marine Corps
'Fête de la Raison' in France: Catholic churches, including the Notre Dame Cathedral, are ceremoniously de-christianized and transformed into Temples of Reason
Kentucky outlaws duelling
Osage Treaty / Treaty of Fort Clark, Osage Nation cedes territory in Missouri and Arkansas to the US
Louis Napoleon banished to America
The passenger ship Stephen Whitney is wrecked in thick fog off the southern coast of Ireland, killing 92 of the 110 on board. The disaster results in the construction the Fastnet Rock lighthouse.
Toronto Argonauts defeat Ottawa FC 9-7, for 1st ORFU Championship
1st Women's Christian Temperance Union meeting held (in Boston)
Granville T Woods patents electric railway
1st CRU championship game: Osgoode Hall defeats Montreal, 45-5
Fred Lugard signs accord with king Lafia "Absalamu" of Nikki
Wilmington, North Carolina Coup D'état: White Supremacists violently overthrow local government, killing and threatening Black leaders
Sailors join the rebellion at Kronstadt naval base in Russia
1st Gideon Bible put in a hotel room
The date of Thomas A. Davis' opening of the San Diego Army and Navy Academy, though the official founding date is November 23, 1910.
Chinese Imperial army recaptures Nanking (blood bath)
41 suffragists are arrested in front of White House
Faure's 2nd Violo Sonate premieres
Western Union Cable Office in North Sydney, Nova Scotia, receives a top-secret coded message from Europe stating that on November 11, 1918, all fighting will cease on land, sea, and in the air
1st observance of National Book Week
American Legion's 1st national convention (Minneapolis)
German ex-crown prince flees Netherlands for Germany
Dion O'Banion, leader of the North Side Gang is assassinated in his flower shop by members of Johnny Torrio's gang, sparking the bloody gang war of the 1920s in Chicago.
Guomindang-regring deallocates seat of Kanton to Wuhan (Hankou)
Black Blizzard snowstorm-duststorm rages from SD to Atlantic
8.3 earthquake shakes East of Shumagin Islands, Alaska
Nobel for literature awarded to Pearl Buck (Good Earth)
Second day of Kristallnacht: pogrom against the Jews in Germany and Austria after assassination of a German diplomat in Paris
Pittsburgh Steelers and Philadelphia Eagles play one of only 4 penalty free games in NFL history; Steelers win 7-3 at Forbes Field, Pittsburgh
Philip Barry's play "Without Love" premieres in NYC
US troops occupy airport of Port-Lyautey, Morocco
US-British troops occupies Oran, Algeria
Ammunition ship USS Mount Hood (AE-11) exploded at Seeadler Harbour, Manus, Admiralty Islands
German riots in Rotterdam/Schiedam 52,000 men sent to Germany
US 9th Army takes Margraten cemetery
College football's #1 Army beats #2 Notre Dame 48-0
Heavy battle in Surabaya between Indonesian nationalists and returning colonialists after World War II, celebrated as Heroes' Day (Hari Pahlawan).
Communists win many seats at French parliamentary election
After 9 years, Cleveland Indians fire manager Lou Boudreau
Clifford Odet's "Country Girl" premieres in NYC
1st long distance telephone call without operator assistance
Giants end their tour of Japan (players got $331 of $3,000 promised)
NFL Cleveland Browns' Don Paul sets club record for longest fumble return with a 89-yard run (and TD), beating Pittsburgh Steelers 24-0
NFL record crowd (102,368), '49ers vs Rams in LA
WUFT TV channel 5 in Gainesville, FL (PBS) begins broadcasting
Braves sign a 25-year lease to play in the new Atlanta stadium
Manneke Pis statue stolen in Brussels
Lunar Orbiter 2 reaches 196-1871 km around Moon
KXNE TV channel 19 in Norfolk, NB (PBS) begins broadcasting
Launch of Zond 6, 2nd unmanned circumlunar & return flight
Luna 17, with unmanned self-propelled Lunokhod 1, is launched
US table tennis team arrived in China
"Ship Ahoy" album by The O'Jays is released (Billboard Album of the Year 1974)
NHL Montreal Canadiens shutout Washington Capitals 11-0
American iron ore freighter ship SS Edmund Fitzgerald, with entire crew of 29, lost in storm on Lake Superior
UN General Assembly approves resolution equating Zionism with racism, by vote of with 72 in favor, 35 against, and 32 abstentions; revoked by resolution in 1991ecember 1991
Utah Supreme Court approves execution of convicted murderer Gary Gilmore
Amsterdam: Red Army Faction terrorists Gert Schneider and Christof Wackernagel arrested
Major Indoor Soccer League officially organized (NYC)
Israel's top negotiators broke away from Middle East peace talks
NY Yankees trade Lyle, Rajsich, McCall, Heath and Ramos to Texas for Righetti, Mirabella, Beniquez, Jemison and Griffin
Train detrailment in Mississauga, Ontario; a 106 car train derails causing the evacuation of 200,000 people
Iraq captures southern port of Khorramshahr
Poland acknowledges Solidarity union
Ernest Thompson's "West Side Waltz" premieres in NYC
IMF lends Mexico $3.8 billion due to threatened bankruptcy
US Federal government shut down
Australia all out for 76 v West Indies at cricket WACA, Holding 6-21
Miami Hurricanes blows 31-0 lead in 3rd quarter lose to Md 42-40

NHL Philadelphia Flyers goaltender Pelle Lindbergh smashes his Porsche into a retaining wall in Somerdale, New Jersey after a team party, suffering mortal injuries with two passengers critically hurt; his blood-alcohol level was over twice the legal limit, his family had him removed from life support when they arrived from Sweden
Bangladeshi Constitution restored
Steve Bedrosian edges Rick Sutcliffe 57-55 to win NL Cy Young Award
China confirms earthquake death toll will rise above current 938
MLB All-Star team beats Japan 3-1 in Tokyo (Game 5 of 7)
NY's MTA announces it may replace tokens with credit card type passes
Bulgarian Communist party president Todor Zhikov (78) resigns
Word Perfect 5.1 is shipped
Chandra Shekhar becomes 8th Prime Minister of India
Lebanon releases 2 French hostages (Camille Sontag & Marcel Coudari)
Bernie Kosar ends NFL record of 308 passes without an interception
Browns set club record for largest lead blown (led 23-0), Philadelphia 32-30
Marty Glickman broadcasts his 1,000th football game
South Africa plays its first international cricket match since 1970, a one-day match against India
Slovakian government of Vladimír Mečiar forms
In Nigeria, playwright and environmental activist Ken Saro-Wiwa along with eight others from the Movement for the Survival of the Ogoni People (Mosop) are hanged by government forces
"Jackie - An American Life" opens at Belasco Theater NYC
An agreement is reached at talks in Marrakech, Morocco, on rules for implementation of the Kyoto climate change treaty
Sri Lankan Tamil Parliamentarian Nadarajah Raviraj assassinated in Colombo.
17 people are killed in a helicopter crash as a result of bad weather in Turkey
20 Syrian troops are killed by suicide bombings in Daara
27 people are killed and dozens injured in a prison conflict in Colombo, Sri Lanka
Eli and Edythe Broad Art Museum opens at Michigan State University in East Lansing, Michigan, designed by Zaha Hadid
Israeli counter strike on Palestinian militants in Gaza kills 5 and injure 30
Spaniard Marc Márquez wins the 2013 MotoGP World Championship to become its youngest ever winner at 20
American human-rights activistEthel Kennedyis awarded the US Presidential Medal of Freedom by Barack Obama
Fourth Republican presidential candidates debate, sponsored by "The Wall Street Journal", held in Milwaukee, Wisconsin
Portugal's minority government is toppled by left-wing opposition 2 weeks after coming to power
BBC removes drama from Christmas line-up after one of its stars, Ed Westwick, accused of rape by two women
Louis C.K. admits the stories of sexual misconduct about him are true
Death toll in demonstrations in Iraq reaches 319, with 15,000 injured since Oct 1 according to Iraqi human rights organization
Ceasefire announced signed between Armenia, Azerbaijan and Russia ending military conflict over Nagorno-Karabakh region after over a month of fighting
Word of the year is "Lockdown" according to Collins English Dictionary
China and the US announce plans to work together on cutting greenhouse gas emissions at the COP26 summit
Afghanistan's ruling Taliban ban women from using public parks and funfairs
Hurricane Nicole makes landfall near Vero Beach, Florida as a category 1 hurricane, causing widespread destruction and at least two deaths
Record for the most expensive US stamp made when a 1918 "Inverted Jenny" sells for $2m (£1.63m) at auction in New York
The Beatles single "Now and Then" hits #1 in the British singles chart, a record 54 years after their last #1 single
Mauritius elections won in a landslide by opposition party Alliance of Change, with Navin Ramgoolam leading the party to 60 out of 64 seats
Next transit of Earth as seen from Mars
René Descarteshas a dream that inspires his "Meditations on First Philosophy"
First American Chess Congress is won byPaul Morphy; beats Louis Paulson, 6-2 for a tournament record of 14 wins, 3 draws and 1 loss

Andrew Carnegieforms Carnegie Corporation for scholarly and charitable works
Arthur Nikisch leads the Berlin Philharmonic in one of the earliest recordings of a complete symphony -Ludwig van Beethoven's 5th

George Bernard Shaw's play "Heartbreak House" premieres in NYC

Emperor Hirohitoof Japan's possession of the Imperial Regalia is publicly confirmed

First NHL penalty shot is awarded to Montreal Canadiens forward Armand Mondou; stopped by Toronto goaltenderGeorge Hainsworthin Maple Leafs' 2-1 win

Walt Disneybegins serving as an informer for the Los Angeles office of the FBI; his job is to report back information on Hollywood subversives.

Nobel Prize for literature awarded toWilliam Faulkner"for his powerful and artistically unique contribution to the modern American novel"
Trygve Lieresigns as 1st Secretary General of the United Nations

"Sesame Street" premieres on PBS TV
Joe Torrewins NL MVP,Vida Bluewins AL MVP
MLB Kansas City Royals release sluggerHarmon Killebrew, ending his 22-year career
PLO leaderYasser Arafataddresses UN in NYC
Larry HolmesKOs Alfredo Evangelist in 7 for heavyweight boxing title
AnchormanDan Ratheralleges cabbie tried to kidnap him, refuses to pay his fare in dispute with cab driver

'Marvelous' Marvin Hagler retains world middleweight boxing title with a 15-round unanimous points decision overRoberto Duránat Caesars Palace, Las Vegas; first time Hagler taken the distance as champion
Columbia Records releasesBruce Springsteenand the E-Street Band "Live/1975-85", a 5 LP box set of concert recordings
Orel Hershiser(23-8) is a unanimous choice as NL Cy Young Award
John Hughes' film "Home Alone" directed by Chris Columbus and starringMacaulay Culkinpremieres in Chicago
Martina NavratilovatiesChris Evertwith 157 pro tennis tournament wins

Disney composerAlan Menkenreceives the 2,442nd star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame
